title: [InferResets] Print field/signal names in diagnostic
body:
The `InferResets` pass currently does not mention any field, signal, or port names in its diagnostics, which can be very confusing if the resets in question are part of bundles or if there are multiple resets on a module.

We should probably add something like a `guessName(FieldRef fieldRef)` function that looks at the value in a field ref, traverses it through any subfield accesses (adding them to name) until it hits some terminal value (like "instance port inst.xyz" or "module port 'reset'"), and then appends the subfield accessors of the field ref. That could give us things like "instance port inst.io.stuff[42].foo.reset" or "module port io.questionableSignals[42].resetA".

The diagnostic printing happens here. If we had a function that makes a best-effort name guess we could just call it here and have the diagnostic actually include names that are helpful to the user:
https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/2c6068496475fc087b77f0718cfad70a472af5e1/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/Transforms/InferResets.cpp#L985-L1018
